content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array of integers. The array has **even length** and contains an **equal number of positive and negative integers**. Chef wants to rearrange the array so that:


Every consecutive pair of numbers has **opposite signs**.
The **relative order** of positive numbers among themselves and negative numbers among themselves is **preserved**.
The array **starts with a positive number**.
Help Chef rearrange his array according to these rules.

**Function Declaration****Function Name**rearrangeArrayrearrangeArrayrearrangeArray – This function rearranges an array of integers.

**Parameters**

arrarrarr : A reference to a vector of integers of size nnn.



Contains both positive and negative integers.
Has an equal number of positive and negative values.



nnn : An integer representing the size of the array



Always even
Satisfies all constraints


**Return Value**

The function does **not** return anything.




It modifies the vector **in-place**, producing a rearranged array that:



Starts with a positive integer
Alternates sign at every position
Preserves the internal order of all positive and all negative elements


**Constraints**
2≤N≤2⋅1052 \leq N \leq 2 \cdot 10^52≤N≤2⋅105
NNN is even
1≤∣A[i]∣≤1051 \leq |A[i]| \leq 10^51≤∣A[i]∣≤105
The array contains **exactly** N/2N/2N/2 positive and N/2N/2N/2 negative integers

Input Format

The first line contains an integer NNN — the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the array elements.


Output Format

For each test case, print the rearranged array (starting with positive and alternating signs) on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The positives in order: [5,2,7,4]
The negatives in order: [-1,-3,-2,-6]
The array already starts with a positive number and alternates signs. Preserving relative order of positives and negatives gives the same array [5,-1,2,-3,7,-2,4,-6].

Explanation:
There is only one positive and one negative number. The array must start with a positive number, so the valid rearranged array is [10,-7].

solution.approach:
Traverse the array once and separate numbers into two lists:

`pos` = list of positive numbers in the order they appear.
`neg` = list of negative numbers in the order they appear.


Since the array length is even and positives = negatives, we can simply **interleave** elements from `pos` and `neg`.

Place `pos[0]` at index 0, `neg[0]` at index 1.
Place `pos[1]`` at index 2, `neg[1]` at index 3.
Continue until the end of the array.


Print the resulting array.
Complexity Analysis**Time Complexity**: O(N)


One pass to split positives/negatives, one pass to merge and print.
**Space Complexity**: O(N)


Extra space for storing positives and negatives separately.
